clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
fall
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
none

History of Present Illness:
Ms. ___ is an ___ year old female with a PMHx of diastolic 
heart failure (EF 55%, ___, paroxysmal AF, Sick sinus 
syndrome, AV block s/p PPM, and myasthenia ___ who presented 
after an unwitnessed fall preceded by chest pain. Her Lifealert 
bracelet was activated. The fire department and her son were 
called and she was found on the floor with a hurt shoulder and 
hip.  
She denies feeling strange before fall. She thinks she stumbled 
and fell due to myasthenia ___. Denies CP. She has a lot of 
shortness of breath which was worse after the fall. She gets 
short of breath after just a few steps at times. Does not 
remember if she felt dizzy or light-headed. Has a "feeling that 
comes over" her and she knows she is going to fall. Denies 
palpitations. +Orthopnea. Occasional PND.  

In the ED initial vitals were: 97.8 90 131/75 18 96% RA  
EKG: V-paced rhythm @ 84  
Labs/studies notable for: No leukocytosis, H+H 11.0/38.0, 
BUN/Creat ___, ProBNP 2576, Negative trop x2, UA 
unremarkable.  

Imaging:  
-CXR: New small bilateral effusions and mild pulmonary edema.  
-NCHCT: No acute intracranial process

Hospital Course:
Patient is an ___ who presents for evaluation of syncope after a 
fall at home.  

#Fall with ?syncope/presyncope- The patient had an unwitnessed 
fall. The patient has baseline dementia, and was unable to 
recall details prior to event (or that it was the reason she was 
in the hospital. She underwent a workup in the ED to assess for 
injuries. NCHCT was negative, as was imaging of the shoulder, 
spine, and hip. The patient was not orthostatic, and was not 
having any symptoms. On the floor, she was monitored on 
telemetry with no events. The EP team was able to interrogate 
her pacemaker (___), and was found to be in atrial 
fibrillation, with a 41% burden since ___. She was also noted 
to have had several second runs of PMT around ___ on ___, 
which is approximately the time of her fall. As the patient was 
asymptomatic in the hospital, she was discharged home with 
primary care, cardiology, and EP follow-up. 

#Acute on chronic diastolic heart failure - EF > 55%. On 
admission, the patient was noted to have crackles, ___ edema, 
elevated JVP concerning for fluid overload. Home Lasix 40 mg PO 
daily had been held at last discharge at was restarted at 10 mg 
daily. Weight on admission (bed) was 264 lbs, up from last 
discharge weight of 255 lbs. She was given prn Lasix, and 
restarted on her home Lasix of 10mg on ___. Her imdur was 
decreased to 30mg daily.

# ___ - On admission, the patient's Cr was elevated to 1.4 up 
from 1.2 on ___. Likely ___ dCHF exacerbation.  It was

reports_summary.excerpt:
INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with heart failure, s/p fall with ?syncope,
found to have elevated d-dimer.  // assess for PE

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of 100 cc of Omnipaque intravenous
contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitte

EXAMINATION:  CT C-SPINE W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ with head strike on blood thinners and neck pain  // bleed or
neck fx?

TECHNIQUE:  Non-contrast helical multidetector CT was performed.Soft tissue
and bone algorithm images were generated.  Coronal and sagittal reformations
were then constructed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 733 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT chest dated ___.
CT C-spine dated ___.

clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
fall
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None

History of Present Illness:
___ year old woman with hx pAF, diastolic CHF, SSS s/p pacer, 
myasthenia ___ presenting s/p fall. She told triage that she 
thinks she fell over a threshold at home. She then told her 
nurse that she didn't fall but she just slipped out of chair to 
floor with strike to ankle and knee. She called life alert, who 
arrived at her home and took her to ___. She denies headstrike 
or LOC states she normally get around the house with a walker on 
her own. Denies neck pain. On interview with MD in the ED, she 
absolutely denies falling and states she came in because she's 
short of breath. No chest pain or tightness. Remarks she is 
having trouble catching her breath. She has had this in the past 
but never this severe. Denies cough, fever or chills.  
 She was seen by her PCP ___ ___, after her family brought her 
in for concern about worsening memory and rash on her legs. They 
report that she has been forgetful with taking her medications. 
They do not feel like she can take care of herself. She refuses 
to move into an assisted living home. They also note that she 
has been progressively more short of breath.  
 Of note, she was seen by dermatology on ___ for rash on her 
legs. She w
[... truncated ...]

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with afib on apixaban, s/p unwitnessed fall.
Decreased reactivity of right pupil  // Evidence of intracranial bleed

contrast.

   1) Sequenced Acquisition 7.0 s, 14.2 cm; CTDIvol = 49.4 mGy (Head) DLP =
702.4 mGy-cm.

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with afib on apixaban, s/p unwitnessed fall.

fracture
